title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
summary: Gabe De Mesa from OpenGov presents a talk on building and scaling AI agents in production for government workflows. The session covers agent architecture, feedback and evaluation, long context handling, monitoring, tool integration, and development practices using Effect-TS and Typescript with Claude and Cursor.
writeup: Gabe De Mesa of OpenGov walks through how the company built and scaled OG Assist, an AI agent system aimed at government workflows. The talk covers the production concerns that distinguish working agents from demos: agent architecture, feedback and evaluation loops, long-context handling, monitoring and observability, and tool integration. It also details the development stack behind the system, using Effect-TS and TypeScript alongside Claude and Cursor. The throughline is what it takes to keep agents reliable once they leave prototyping and serve real users.
